prose:
Method–provenance summary
• Property: HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans‐style orbital gap), units eV  
• Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fitted RI in PySCF)  
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates  
  – file: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz  
  – single-point only; no geometry optimisation (notation: DFT//xyz)  
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvent)  
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet, RKS)  
• Backend: PySCF (frontier-orbitals skill), SCF converged in 10 cycles  
• Convergence / caveats: SCF converged; no warnings reported by the engine.  
• Live run log: the skill streamed its stdout/err to a time-stamped
  frontier_orbitals_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory
  (tail ‑f to inspect); JSON result written to
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpo8lzrv_o/live.json.

Results (selected levels in eV)
– HOMO-2  = −8.0425195  
– HOMO-1  = −7.4731156  
– HOMO    = −7.2525992  
– LUMO    = −1.1127860  
– LUMO+1  = −0.67231536  
– HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.139813146380714 eV  ← headline value

Koopmans-derived reactivity indices
– Vertical ionisation potential (IP)  = 7.2525992 eV  
– Vertical electron affinity (EA)     = 1.112786 eV  
– Electronegativity χ                 = 4.1826926 eV  
– Chemical hardness η                = 3.0699066 eV  
– Electrophilicity index ω           = 2.8494218 eV  
– Chemical softness S                 = 0.32574281 eV⁻¹

No warnings were returned by the engine. All integrity checks passed (integrity.status = “ok”; trustworthy = true).

This is a computed result; no experimental comparison has been requested or provided.
